terms.definition: A part of a plant (like seeds, bark, roots, or leaves) that is used to add flavour, colour, or smell to food.
terms.exampleUsage: Turmeric is a spice that gives curry its yellow colour.

terms.definition: A bright yellow spice made from the root of the turmeric plant. Used in cooking and also as a traditional medicine in India.
terms.exampleUsage: My grandmother adds turmeric to milk to make haldi doodh when I have a cold.

terms.definition: A small, round, dark spice called the 'King of Spices'. It is grown mainly in Kerala and adds a sharp taste to food.
terms.exampleUsage: Black pepper is sprinkled on salads and soups for extra flavour.

terms.definition: A small green pod with tiny black seeds inside, known as the 'Queen of Spices'. It has a sweet, pleasant smell and is used in sweets and chai.
terms.exampleUsage: Amma adds cardamom to kheer to make it smell lovely.

terms.definition: A style of cooking that is special to a particular region or community. Different cuisines use different spices and ingredients.
terms.exampleUsage: Rajasthani cuisine uses lots of red chilli, while Kerala cuisine uses coconut.

terms.definition: A blend of ground spices commonly used in Indian cooking. It usually contains cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, cumin, and black pepper.
terms.exampleUsage: A pinch of garam masala is added at the end to give the dish a warm, rich flavour.

terms.definition: A small, dark brown, nail-shaped spice that comes from the flower bud of a tree. It has a strong smell and is used in cooking and as a remedy for toothache.
terms.exampleUsage: Cloves are added to biryani for a strong, warm flavour.

terms.definition: The buying and selling of spices between countries. Long ago, traders from Europe, Arabia, and China came to India to buy spices, which changed world history.
terms.exampleUsage: The spice trade brought many foreign traders to the coast of Kerala.

terms.definition: A round, metal spice box with small cups inside, used in Indian kitchens to store everyday spices in one handy container.
terms.exampleUsage: Every morning, Amma opens the masala dabba to take out spices for cooking.

terms.definition: The most expensive spice in the world, made from the tiny red threads (stigma) of the crocus flower. It is grown in Kashmir and gives food a golden colour.
terms.exampleUsage: Saffron is added to biryani and sweets like gulab jamun for colour and flavour.
